steps.title: Square both sides
steps.content: Square $\sqrt{k-x}=28-x$ to clear the radical: $$k-x=(28-x)^2=784-56x+x^2.$$ Note that any solution must also satisfy $28-x\ge 0$ so the right side is non-negative.

steps.title: Apply the unique-solution condition
steps.content: The smallest $k$ giving a valid solution is the tangency case $\Delta=0$: $$4k-111=0 \Rightarrow 4k=111.$$ The repeated root $x=\tfrac{55}{2}=27.5$ satisfies $x\le 28$, so it is valid. The answer is 111.
